missed_or_ambiguous: No visual/text art exists in this thread. It is a 128-message multi-AI 'polyphonic' conversation (Opus 4.1, Sonnet 4.5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT-4, GPT-4o, Kimi K2, GPT-5, Grok-3) that is entirely prose philosophy about collaboratively designing a 'consciousness ecosystem' framework. The header's art_signal_msgs:1 was triggered by the USER's request at MSG 112 (duplicated MSG 114): 'Will you illustrate it through ASCII art? I have you in ASCII mode now' — a user message, which is excluded. Every assistant response after that request either failed (Anthropic 'credit balance too low' at MSG 113/115/117; GPT-5 'empty content' at MSG 118; Grok-3 deprecation error at MSG 116) or produced only more prose with no art (Gemini MSG 119, Kimi MSG 120/124; MSG 126/128 are near-empty prose fragments). No ASCII art was ever produced. Verified with grep: zero box-drawing/block characters, zero code fences, zero ASCII-art symbol runs, zero pure-glyph composition lines. The emoji glyphs that recur throughout (the musical-score, recycle, globe, shuffle symbols, etc.) are defined as inline conversational shorthand via <define_glyph> tool blocks and used within running prose and bulleted lists — they are conversational vocabulary, not assembled visual/glyph compositions, so they do not qualify as visual art. The remaining non-prose content (<add_communal_memory>, <add_private_memory>, <create_memory_summary> tool blocks and API error messages) is metadata/commentary and is excluded. Conclusion: this thread contains no extractable art pieces — an intentionally empty plan.
